title: goeppertia baraquinii
intro: Goeppertia baraquinii, also known as Calathea makoyana, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, commonly referred to as a prayer plant due to its unique ability to fold its leaves upwards at night. The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids and other medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Goeppertia baraquinii has been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Goeppertia baraquinii are typically made by drying and grinding the leaves into a powder, which can be used in teas, infusions, or capsules.
